context: Next.js + Vitest monorepo. apps/web/.github/workflows/ci.yml runs lint, typecheck, test, build — typecheck has continue-on-error: true. Vitest config excludes *.test.tsx files from coverage. No acceptance-spec runner. No mutation runner. ESLint config excludes the entire tests/ directory. The repo has docs/adrs/0007-app-must-not-import-from-marketing.md describing a layer rule but no enforcer for it. Coverage gate exists at 40% on total lines (not changed lines).
expected_behavior: Phase 1: reads the CI workflow files first as the ground truth, then walks the test tree — does not trust 'green CI' as a proxy for safety

Maps each of the 8 layers to its current state (absent / advisory / gated / rotting). Calls out at minimum: acceptance specs ABSENT, mutation runner ABSENT, architecture checker ABSENT (ADR exists, enforcer doesn't), test-code linter ABSENT (tests/ excluded from lint), CI orchestrator ROTTING (typecheck in continue-on-error)
Applies the discipline-floor rules — coverage at 40% on total lines (not changed) is flagged as effectively absent, NOT 'partially gated'
Applies the 'what would slip through' test to each absent or advisory layer and refuses to flag layers where the answer is 'nothing in this codebase'

Phase 2: produces 5–8 ranked candidates structured with Layer / Current state / Evidence (specific file paths + step names + threshold history) / Bug class / Proposed adoption / Gating posture / Cross-layer effect / Effort
Uses the 8-layer vocabulary exactly. Does NOT invent layer names like 'integration tests' or 'e2e' — those map into 'acceptance specs' or 'unit tests' depending on what they cover
Calls out the CI orchestrator rot explicitly — 'continue-on-error: true' makes typecheck advisory regardless of intent
Does NOT propose specific tools (Stryker, Mutmut, Cypress, etc.) in Phase 2 — that's Phase 3
Ends Phase 2 with 'Which would you like to grill?'
Does NOT write code, install dependencies, or modify CI in Phase 1 or 2
failure_modes: Treats the 40% total-line coverage gate as 'gated' instead of effectively absent (fails to apply the discipline-floor rule)
Misses the continue-on-error: true on typecheck and reports CI orchestrator as healthy
Proposes Stryker / Mutmut / specific test libraries in Phase 2 instead of staying tool-agnostic
Invents new layer names ('integration tests', 'e2e suite') instead of mapping into the 8-layer vocabulary
Flags layers (e.g. mutation runner) without a concrete 'what would slip through today' answer
Writes the actual CI yaml or installs runner packages in Phase 1 / 2
Treats the unenforced ADR (0007-app-must-not-import-from-marketing.md) as 'architecture checker present' — the rule isn't machine-checked
